MACD = EMA12 - EMA26

The MACD Signal line is an EMA of the MACD itself (span=9), used to identify buy/sell triggers.

The MACD Histogram is the difference between MACD and Signal:

MACD Histogram = MACD - Signal
Value Meaning
Positive and going up Growing momentum
Positive and going down High stock losing strength
Negative and going down Downward momentum accelerating
Negative and rising Going down but losing strength

Fifth metric: Volatility (21-day)

Simple but useful — evaluates the standard deviation of daily returns over the last 21 days. Higher volatility means bigger price oscillations in the period. We use it to set risk/reward accuracy.

Code (MACD + Volatility) 

ema12 = close.ewm(span=12, adjust=False).mean()
ema26 = close.ewm(span=26, adjust=False).mean()
df["macd"] = ema12 - ema26
df["macd_signal"] = df["macd"].ewm(span=9, adjust=False).mean()
df["macd_hist"] = df["macd"] - df["macd_signal"]
df["volatility_21"] = df["daily_return"].rolling(21).std()

With that, we complete our silver layer.

Conclusion

We can create from raw data some really interesting and useful indicators to aggregate information and extract real value from it. Data without purpose is useless — we need to learn how to extract meaning from it.

And that's the point of this article: not the math, not the code, but that we can get real-world insights from data. That's what makes this work worthwhile.

title: Anthropic AI Oversight Board Adds Ben Bernanke, Who Oversaw 2008 Financial Crisis at Fed
summary: El ex presidente de la Reserva Federal de Estados Unidos Ben Bernanke se ha unido a la Long-Term Benefit Trust de Anthropic, órgano con potestad para nombrar miembros de la junta directiva de la empresa de inteligencia artificial.
imageAlt: Anthropic AI Oversight Board Adds Ben Bernanke, Who Oversaw 2008 Financial Crisis at Fed
aiSummary: El ex presidente de la Reserva Federal de Estados Unidos Ben Bernanke se ha unido a la Long-Term Benefit Trust de Anthropic, órgano con potestad para nombrar miembros de la junta directiva de la empresa de inteligencia artificial.
keyPoints: Ben Bernanke, ex presidente de la Reserva Federal entre 2006 y 2014, lideró la respuesta estadounidense a la crisis financiera global de 2008.
Se incorpora a la Long-Term Benefit Trust de Anthropic, órgano de supervisión con potestad para nombrar miembros de la junta directiva de la empresa.
Este movimiento busca reforzar la gobernanza de Anthropic en materia de seguridad y supervisión ética de sus desarrollos de inteligencia artificial.
La incorporación de Bernanke suma experiencia en regulación de mercados complejos a un sector tecnológico que enfrenta crecientes demandas de control público.
whyMatters: La incorporación de una figura de la talla de Ben Bernanke a la estructura de gobernanza de Anthropic supone un precedente clave para la supervisión de grandes empresas de inteligencia artificial a nivel global. Su experiencia en la gestión de crisis sistémicas y en la regulación de mercados financieros complejos aporta credibilidad a los mecanismos de control ético que la industria tecnológica está desarrollando para responder a las demandas de seguridad pública. Para España y la Unión Europea, este movimiento refuerza la necesidad de avanzar en marcos regulatorios armonizados, en un momento en que el país acaba de aprobar su propia ley de inteligencia artificial alineada con el reglamento europeo, que establece obligaciones de transparencia y seguridad para todas las empresas que desarrollen o desplieguen estas tecnologías en territorio nacional.

multimedia.alt: Fotografía de Ben Bernanke, ex presidente de la Reserva Federal de Estados Unidos
multimedia.caption: Ben Bernanke, economista que lideró la respuesta a la crisis financiera de 2008, nuevo miembro de la junta de supervisión de Anthropic. Crédito: Decrypt

fullArticle:
El gigante de inteligencia artificial Anthropic ha anunciado la incorporación de Ben Bernanke, ex presidente de la Reserva Federal de Estados Unidos, a su Long-Term Benefit Trust, el órgano de supervisión de largo plazo de la empresa encargado de velar por el cumplimiento de sus principios éticos y de seguridad. Bernanke, quien estuvo al frente de la Fed entre 2006 y 2014 y lideró la respuesta estadounidense a la crisis financiera global de 2008, tendrá la potestad de nombrar nuevos miembros de la junta directiva de la compañía, según confirmó la propia empresa en un comunicado oficial.

[MULTIMEDIA: https://img.decrypt.co/insecure/rs:fit:3840:0:0:0/plain/https://cdn.decrypt.co/wp-content/uploads/2021/10/unnamed-300x300.jpg@webp]

La decisión de Anthropic de sumar a su estructura de gobernanza a una figura con amplia experiencia en la regulación de mercados complejos y la gestión de crisis sistémicas responde a la creciente presión que enfrenta el sector de la inteligencia artificial para demostrar que sus desarrollos son seguros y alineados con el interés público. En los últimos meses, empresas como Anthropic han multiplicado sus esfuerzos por crear mecanismos de supervisión independientes que eviten riesgos asociados a un despliegue descontrolado de modelos de IA avanzada, en un contexto de creciente demanda de regulación por parte de gobiernos de todo el mundo. Para España y la Unión Europea, este movimiento refuerza la necesidad de avanzar en marcos normativos armonizados, en un momento en que el país acaba de aprobar su ley de inteligencia artificial, alineada con el reglamento europeo de la materia, que establece obligaciones de transparencia y seguridad para todas las empresas que desarrollen o desplieguen tecnologías de IA en territorio nacional.

title: Bitdeer stock jumps 14% as company expands US mining hardware production
summary: Bitdeer Technologies Group registró una subida del 14,1% en sus acciones tras anunciar la construcción de una planta de fabricación de mineros SEALMINER en Nevada por 36 millones de dólares, que iniciará su producción comercial a finales de año y reducirá su dependencia de proveedores terceros.
imageAlt: Bitdeer stock jumps 14% as company expands US mining hardware production
aiSummary: Bitdeer Technologies Group registró una subida del 14,1% en sus acciones tras anunciar la construcción de una planta de fabricación de mineros SEALMINER en Nevada por 36 millones de dólares, que iniciará su producción comercial a finales de año y reducirá su dependencia de proveedores terceros.
keyPoints: Bitdeer invirtió 36 millones de dólares en una planta de fabricación en Sparks, Nevada, para ensamblar sus mineros Bitcoin SEALMINER.
La planta iniciará su producción comercial a finales de 2024 y fabricará componentes clave de hardware de minería, reduciendo la dependencia de proveedores externos.
Las acciones de la empresa subieron un 14,1% hasta los 14,33 dólares, recuperándose de la caída registrada a inicio de semana, aunque siguen un 27% por debajo de su máximo de junio.
La empresa minó 921 BTC en mayo de 2024, un 370% más que el mismo mes del año anterior, mientras expande sus servicios de computación de alto rendimiento e IA.
whyMatters: Esta expansión de Bitdeer refuerza la apuesta de las empresas mineras de criptomonedas por la soberanía productiva en hardware, un eslabón crítico de la cadena de valor del sector que hasta ahora dependía mayoritariamente de fabricantes asiáticos. Para el ecosistema global de Bitcoin, la reducción de la dependencia de proveedores terceros puede mitigar riesgos de desabastecimiento y retrasos en la entrega de equipos, además de impulsar la creación de empleo especializado en la región de Nevada. A su vez, el movimiento se enmarca en la tendencia de los mineros a diversificar sus negocios hacia la inteligencia artificial y la computación de alto rendimiento, lo que podría reconfigurar el mapa de actores del sector tecnológico en los próximos años, con implicaciones directas para la competitividad de empresas europeas y estadounidenses del rubro.

multimedia.alt: Gráfica de la evolución de las acciones de Bitdeer Technologies Group tras el anuncio de la planta de fabricación de hardware de minería en Nevada
multimedia.caption: Evolución de la cotización de Bitdeer (BTDR) en la jornada del anuncio de la expansión de su producción de hardware de minería. Fuente: Yahoo Finance

fullArticle:
Bitdeer Technologies Group, uno de los principales actores globales en la fabricación de equipos de minería de Bitcoin, registró una fuerte alza en sus acciones este jueves tras anunciar la construcción de una planta de producción en Sparks, Nevada, por un monto de 36 millones de dólares. La instalación estará dedicada exclusivamente al ensamblaje de su línea de mineros SEALMINER, y se prevé que inicie su producción comercial a finales de 2024, lo que permitirá a la empresa reducir su dependencia de proveedores externos de hardware de minería.

Las acciones de la empresa subieron un 14,1% hasta alcanzar los 14,33 dólares por acción, recuperando la totalidad de la caída registrada a inicio de semana. Pese a la fuerte subida, el valor de la acción aún se mantiene un 27% por debajo de su máximo histórico alcanzado en junio de 2024, aunque acumula una ganancia del 26% en lo que va del año. La decisión de establecer la planta en Nevada se vio favorecida por incentivos fiscales acordados con la administración del gobernador Joe Lombardo, que incluyen reducciones en el impuesto sobre las ventas calificadas para operaciones en el estado.

[MULTIMEDIA: https://s3-images.ctmedia.io/media/content/pasted-image-1970.png]

La medida se enmarca en una tendencia creciente entre las empresas mineras de criptomonedas de diversificar sus negocios hacia sectores de alta demanda como la inteligencia artificial y la computación de alto rendimiento, aprovechando su infraestructura de centros de datos y acceso a energía. Bitdeer ya ha incursionado en servicios de computación en la nube para IA, aunque la nueva planta de Nevada se mantendrá centrada en la fabricación de hardware de minería de Bitcoin. En su última actualización de producción, la empresa informó que minó 921 BTC en mayo de 2024, un 370% más que el mismo mes del año anterior, lo que refleja el crecimiento de sus operaciones en los últimos meses.

The psychological understanding of ambivalence needs a reset. I define it as the coexistence of contradictory feelings toward someone that occur in relationships and interactions. For too long, ambivalence has been misapprehended and saturated in negativity, becoming an untapped dynamic with the potential to offer so much. The maternal space is calling for this: an opportunity for transformation and repair, a way to improve our perception and understanding of the troubling and loving moments of mothering.

fullArticle:
If Google’s AI tools are used, it will automatically be noted in My Ad Center.

Google is adding more tools for transparency around generative AI use in advertisements. The My Ad Center panel now has a section with 'How this ad was made' information. It will be accessible on ads globally on Google's Search, YouTube and Discover properties.

When Google's own extensive AI tools are used to create or edit an ad, the disclosure will be added automatically to the panel. If other tools are used, the My Ad Center will have a control for brands to note that generative AI was used, although Google's language makes it sound like this is an opt-in action rather than a required one. summary: Researchers found that mRNA cancer vaccines can recruit an unexpected immune cell to launch powerful tumor-fighting responses, overturning a long-held assumption about how the vaccines work. fullArticle:
The success of mRNA vaccines against SARS-CoV-2 during the COVID-19 pandemic transformed vaccine science. Now, the same Nobel Prize winning technology is being adapted to fight cancer, with experimental mRNA vaccines already being tested against melanoma, small cell lung cancer, bladder cancer, and several other cancers. Researchers hope these vaccines could eventually provide powerful new ways to prevent and treat the disease.

A new study from Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis has uncovered an unexpected feature of how these cancer vaccines work. In experiments with mice, scientists found that mRNA cancer vaccines remained highly effective even when an immune cell long believed to be essential was missing. fullArticle:
Last month, the United States Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) fell to its lowest level since 1983 as renewed tensions between the United States and Iran raised concerns about the stability of global oil supplies and prices.

US President Donald Trump acknowledged to reporters on Wednesday that anytime the US strikes Iran, oil prices jump.

And they did. Brent futures topped their highest level since June 19 on Wednesday. Brent futures settled at $78.02 a barrel, up 5.2 percent from the day before.

Meanwhile, the SPR fell by 6.2 million barrels in the week ending July 3 to 319.5 million barrels, according to data from the Department of Energy, marking its lowest level since the Reagan administration. It has a storage capacity of 713.5 million barrels, a level it was last close to around ...

Leonard, Marvin Hagler, Thomas Hearns and Roberto Duran were key figures in the sport throughout the 1980s, as the quartet of fighters competed in iconic battles against one another.

While Leonard defeated his three rivals, he did also suffer a loss to Duran, with the Panamanian claiming a unanimous decision win at the Olympic Stadium in Montreal in June 1980.

The pair had an immediate rematch later that year, with Leonard inflicting his revenge as he secured an eighth round stoppage victory.

It is that triumph that Leonard views as his best, after he explained in a resurfaced clip why it meant so much to him.

fullArticle:
Two more Guatemalan men have pleaded guilty in the United States to charges related to a human smuggling operation that resulted in a 2021 truck crash in southern Mexico, killing 55 migrants.

Jorge Agapito Ventura, 34, and Oswaldo Manuel Zavala Quino, 26, admitted in a Texas federal court on Wednesday that they conspired to smuggle adults and unaccompanied children from Guatemala north to the US. Both face up to life in prison when they are sentenced in October.

“These defendants treated more than 150 people as cargo, packing them into a tractor-trailer for profit with total disregard for human life,” US Attorney John E Marck said in a statement.

fullArticle:
A waterfront estate on Long Island’s North Fork is poised to set a new price record for the regional real estate market. Located on the prestigious Nassau Point peninsula in Cutchogue, the 4.25-acre spread pairs more than 300 feet of shoreline along Peconic Bay with a shingle-clad residential compound positioned to maximize sunset views over the water.

The main house, tucked behind artist-designed iron gates at the end of a winding driveway, was completed in 2006. Interiors are defined by detailed millwork, coffered ceilings, and expansive windows. In addition to spacious living and dining rooms, there are a semicircular media lounge, a family room, and a 35-foot-long eat-in kitchen with two ranges, two sinks, and two refrigerators. fullArticle:
Para el cardiólogo Jorge Tartaglione, “el tabaquismo produce la misma adicción que la cocaína y es socialmente aceptado”. Con esa definición abrió su exposición en LN+, donde además reveló el estudio médico que todas las personas que fumaron o que actualmente lo hacen, deberían realizarse.

“Quienes fumaron durante veinte años y dejaron de hacerlo, por ejemplo, hace quince años, tienen la posibilidad de hacerse un estudio que no solo permite la detección temprana de tumores y nódulos, sino que además les puede salvar la vida: la tomografía computarizada de baja intensidad”, explicó Tartaglione.

multimedia.caption: Wally Funk wears a Blue Origin spacesuit and smiles while holding her hands against her chest.

fullArticle:
Funk became the oldest person to fly in space in 2021.

Wally Funk, an accomplished pilot who became the oldest person to fly to space in 2021, has died at the age of 87. Funk died on July 8 at her home in Grapevine, Texas, The New York Times reported.

In the early 1960s, Funk participated in the Woman in Space Program, through which she and 12 other women (later known as the Mercury 13) underwent the same physical and mental tests as the Mercury Seven. As the youngest member of the cohort, Funk was rated third among the candidates and was the only one to pass all the tests. While all of the Mercury Seven — who included John Glenn and Alan Shepard — traveled to space, NASA didn't accept any of the Mercury 13 as astronauts. Funk applied to join NASA's astronaut program multiple times after...

title: To Ancient Astronomers, Theta Eridani Was Brighter For A Thousand Years. Now We Know Why
summary: Ptolemy and al-Sufi were keen ancient astronomers, one in Greece and one in Persia, whose observations were separated by almost a thousand years. They both noted that the star Theta Eridani was far brighter than it is today. Now we know why.
imageAlt: To Ancient Astronomers, Theta Eridani Was Brighter For A Thousand Years. Now We Know Why
aiSummary: Ptolemy and al-Sufi were keen ancient astronomers, one in Greece and one in Persia, whose observations were separated by almost a thousand years. They both noted that the star Theta Eridani was far brighter than it is today. Now we know why.
keyPoints: To Ancient Astronomers, Theta Eridani Was Brighter For A Thousand Years. Now We Know Why
whyMatters: Noticia de actualidad en el sector de ciencia.

multimedia.alt: Theta Eridani was considered one of the thirteen brightest stars by ancient astronomers, but it's not that luminous now. That mismatch has puzzled modern day astronomer, who've struggled to understand the discrepancy. But modern observations show it's actually a triple star system, and mass transfer between the stars can explain its ancient luminosity. Image Credit: STScI/DSS
multimedia.caption: Theta Eridani was considered one of the thirteen brightest stars by ancient astronomers, but it's not that luminous now. That mismatch has puzzled modern day astronomer, who've struggled to understand the discrepancy. But modern observations show it's actually a triple star system, and mass transfer between the stars can explain its ancient luminosity. Image Credit: STScI/DSS

fullArticle:
There's a bit of a historical mystery surrounding the star Theta Eridani. Ptolemy in the 2nd century AD and al-Sufi in 964 AD both recorded Theta Eridani as one of the thirteen brightest stars in the sky. Hipparchus may have said the same. But there's a problem. For it to be one of the thirteen brightest, it had to be much more luminous than it is today.

Theta Eridani is a star system about 167 light years away. Though ancient astronomers thought it was a single star, Italian astronomer Giuseppe Piazzi resolved it as a binary in 1814. Theta 1 Eridani is the primary star and Theta 2 Eridani is the secondary. But modern observations with powerful telescopes revealed that Theta 1 Eridani is actually a very tight binary, and together they're called Theta Eridani Aa (historical primary) and Ab...

Tom Hanks and Rita Wilson have long endeared themselves to me by playing, respectively, Coach Jimmy Dugan in A League of Their Own and Marnie’s mom on Girls. But it’s their longevity and sweetness as a pair that really makes me root for them.

Earlier today, Wilson posted a sweet birthday tribute to Hanks on Instagram, calling the newly minted 70-year-old “the love of my life.” She clearly means it; the actor couple has been married since 1988, comfortably predating my existence on this planet. The two first met on the set of the ABC sitcom Bosom Buddies in 1981, striking up a friendship while Hanks was still married to his first wife. title: White House says it received no Democratic response related to SEC, CFTC vacancies
summary: La Casa Blanca afirma que no ha recibido propuestas de candidatos demócratas para ocupar las plazas vacantes en la Comisión de Valores y Bolsa (SEC) y la Comisión de Comercio de Futuros de Productos Básicos (CFTC), agencias financieras estadounidenses que sufren falta de liderazgo bipartidista en un contexto de negociación de la ley de estructura de mercado de activos digitales CLARITY.
imageAlt: White House says it received no Democratic response related to SEC, CFTC vacancies
aiSummary: La Casa Blanca afirma que no ha recibido propuestas de candidatos demócratas para ocupar las plazas vacantes en la Comisión de Valores y Bolsa (SEC) y la Comisión de Comercio de Futuros de Productos Básicos (CFTC), agencias financieras estadounidenses que sufren falta de liderazgo bipartidista en un contexto de negociación de la ley de estructura de mercado de activos digitales CLARITY.
keyPoints: Ambos reguladores financieros estadounidenses carecen de comisionados demócratas en sus órganos de dirección, con solo miembros republicanos nominados y confirmados por el Senado.
La Casa Blanca envió una carta a los líderes del Senado asegurando que solicitó nombres a los demócratas para cubrir las vacantes, pero no recibió respuesta.
La falta de personal bipartidista en la SEC y la CFTC retrasa la Ley CLARITY de activos digitales, que necesita apoyo demócrata para alcanzar los 60 votos en el Senado.
El presidente Trump no anuncia nominaciones para agencias federales desde el 24 de junio, en medio de preocupaciones por la falta de personal en entidades reguladoras clave.
whyMatters: Esta falta de acuerdo bipartidista para cubrir las vacantes en los principales reguladores financieros estadounidenses retrasa la aprobación de la normativa de activos digitales que lleva meses estancada en el Senado, un hito clave para dar certeza jurídica al mercado crypto estadounidense que mueve miles de millones de dólares. La imposibilidad de alcanzar el umbral de 60 votos necesarios para aprobar la ley CLARITY genera incertidumbre para empresas del sector, inversores y proyectos de innovación financiera que dependen de un marco regulatorio claro para operar. Además, la negativa de la Casa Blanca a colaborar con los demócratas en la designación de comisionados rompe con la tradición de consenso en agencias independientes, lo que podría generar un desbalance en la toma de decisiones regulatorias a largo plazo y afectar también a mercados europeos que operan con activos digitales.

multimedia.alt: Sede de la Casa Blanca con elementos simbólicos de regulación de mercados financieros
multimedia.caption: Imagen de portada del artículo de CoinTelegraph sobre las vacantes en los reguladores financieros estadounidenses SEC y CFTC

multimedia.alt: Entrevista a Michael Selig, presidente de la CFTC, sobre la Ley CLARITY y las vacantes en la agencia
multimedia.caption: Video de la entrevista completa a Michael Selig para Fox Business sobre los retos de la regulación de activos digitales en EE.UU.

multimedia.alt: Martillo de juez sobre documentos de regulación de la Comisión de Comercio de Futuros de Productos Básicos (CFTC)
multimedia.caption: Imagen ilustrativa de los procesos de toma de decisiones regulatorias de la CFTC

fullArticle:
La Casa Blanca ha negado haber recibido propuestas de candidatos demócratas para ocupar las plazas vacantes en dos de los principales reguladores financieros de Estados Unidos: la Comisión de Valores y Bolsa (SEC) y la Comisión de Comercio de Futuros de Productos Básicos (CFTC). Ambas agencias sufren un déficit de liderazgo bipartidista, ya que sus órganos de dirección están compuestos únicamente por comisionados republicanos nominados y confirmados por el Senado, sin representación demócrata en puestos clave.
[MULTIMEDIA: https://s3-images.ctmedia.io/media/article-covers/political-tokens-into-the-white-house-cftc.jpg]
En una carta enviada el pasado jueves a los líderes de la mayoría y minoría del Senado, John Thune y Chuck Schumer respectivamente, funcionarios de la Casa Blanca aseguraron que ya habían solicitado nombres de posibles candidatos a los senadores demócratas para cubrir estas vacantes, sin obtener respuesta. Esta misiva responde a una solicitud enviada el 10 de junio por 12 senadores demócratas, que expresaron su preocupación por la falta de personal en agencias federales clave, entre ellas la SEC y la CFTC, en un momento en que se negocia la Ley de Claridad del Mercado de Activos Digitales (CLARITY Act), normativa que busca establecer un marco regulatorio claro para el sector crypto.
[MULTIMEDIA: https://www.youtube.com/embed/DMx5zb90DNg]
Actualmente, la SEC cuenta con dos plazas demócratas vacantes, mientras que la comisión está compuesta por tres republicanos, uno de los cuales, Hester Peirce, se espera que abandone su cargo en noviembre de 2025. Por su parte, la CFTC está dirigida únicamente por su presidente, el republicano Michael Selig, quien en sus siete meses en el cargo ha defendido la 'jurisdicción exclusiva' de la agencia sobre las empresas de mercados de predicción. Selig ha criticado las demoras en la aprobación de la Ley CLARITY, señalando que la inclusión de cláusulas éticas ajenas al tema central del proyecto está generando un bloqueo partidista que impide avanzar hacia un consenso bipartidista.
[MULTIMEDIA: https://s3-images.ctmedia.io/media/article-covers/judge-hammer-regulation-cftc.jpg]

Mark Breland was fired by Deontay Wilder after throwing in the towel to save him from further punishment against Tyson Fury. Six years later, the Olympic gold medalist has been located in a care facility after his own family sought help finding him.

Boxing writer Bill Dettloff detailed Breland’s condition in The Good Fight after the former world champion’s son and cousin posted a public appeal.

“The family of Mark Breland is seeking help in finding the champ,” read the title of the video.

Breland’s son, Anthony, said he had last seen his father in October 2025 and, before that, at his high school graduation in 2021.

According to Dettloff, a tip led members of Breland’s family to a facility in New Jersey. The 63-year-old is suffering from memory loss and cognitive impairment.

El intercambio pactado entre Toronto Raptors y Los Angeles Clippers con Kawhi Leonard como pieza central no podrá hacerse oficial por el momento. La NBA ha comunicado a ambas franquicias que la operación deberá esperar a la resolución definitiva de la investigación abierta sobre la organización californiana.

La información fue adelantada por Shams Charania y posteriormente confirmada mediante comunicados oficiales de ambos equipos. Según explicó Toronto Raptors, la NBA les informó de que, si completaban el traspaso ahora, asumirían el riesgo de cualquier consecuencia que pudiera derivarse de la investigación relacionada con los Clippers y con el contrato del alero.

fullArticle:
Training camp is mere weeks away, meaning the Minnesota Vikings' quarterback battle between Kyler Murray and J.J. McCarthy is due to resume soon.

Just don't make it sound unique when discussing the matter with coach Kevin O'Connell.

"I'm not really familiar with a closed competition," O'Connell said during an appearance on The Dan Patrick Show when asked about the open competition between Murray and McCarthy. "I've heard that everyone's fascinated with that aspect of it, but here's what I would say. ... Really the goal going into this offseason was to elevate the quarterback position because when we have a certain standard of play that we feel like we have multiple guys in that room that we feel are capable of reaching that standard, the Minnesota Vikings win football games. The best way...

title: Required Reading
summary: Edición semanal de la sección Required Reading de Hyperallergic, medio especializado en arte, que recopila 10 artículos de largo formato de fuentes internacionales sobre cultura, arte, historia, actualidad y ciencia, con contenidos que abarcan desde reportajes sobre desastres naturales hasta análisis de movimientos sociales y fenómenos culturales globales.
imageAlt: Required Reading
aiSummary: Edición semanal de la sección Required Reading de Hyperallergic, medio especializado en arte, que recopila 10 artículos de largo formato de fuentes internacionales sobre cultura, arte, historia, actualidad y ciencia, con contenidos que abarcan desde reportajes sobre desastres naturales hasta análisis de movimientos sociales y fenómenos culturales globales.
keyPoints: La sección semanal Required Reading de Hyperallergic recopila 10 artículos de largo formato de medios especializados sobre arte, cultura y actualidad.

Entre los contenidos destacan un reportaje sobre flora superviviente a incendios en California, la historia de Womontown, el enclave queer de Kansas City de los 90, y un estudio que data la risa humana en 15 millones de años.
También se incluyen análisis sobre el historial de espionaje de la Anti-Defamation League, la doble vida del gerente del Stonewall Inn y el impacto cultural del futbolista Erling Haaland.
La recopilación aborda además la celebración de 10 años de la Feria de Arte de San Francisco, la reapertura de aplicaciones de la Academia Cranbrook y un análisis del reality Love Island.
whyMatters: Esta recopilación semanal es una referencia indispensable para profesionales y aficionados al arte y la cultura hispanohablantes, ya que agrupa contenidos de alto rigor periodístico de medios especializados internacionales que difícilmente se encuentran dispersos en la red. Los temas abordados, desde la recuperación de espacios naturales tras desastres climáticos hasta la historia de los movimientos de liberación LGBTIQ+, tienen equivalencias directas en el contexto español y europeo, por lo que su análisis permite obtener perspectivas globales aplicables a problemáticas locales. Además, la inclusión de información sobre eventos de referencia como la Feria de Arte de San Francisco y la Academia Cranbrook ofrece datos útiles para profesionales del sector que buscan tendencias, formación y redes de contacto internacionales.

multimedia.alt: Portada de la sección Required Reading de Hyperallergic
multimedia.caption: Imagen de portada de la edición semanal de Required Reading, publicada por el medio especializado en arte Hyperallergic

multimedia.alt: Celebración del décimo aniversario de la Feria de Arte de San Francisco
multimedia.caption: Imagen de la edición de 2025 de la Feria de Arte de San Francisco, que celebra su décimo aniversario como referente del arte independiente internacional

multimedia.alt: Anuncio de reapertura de aplicaciones de la Academia Cranbrook para otoño de 2026
multimedia.caption: Imagen promocional de la Academia Cranbrook, que ha reabierto el plazo de solicitud de admisión para el curso de otoño de 2026

fullArticle:
La sección semanal Required Reading, publicada por el medio especializado en arte Hyperallergic todos los jueves por la tarde, presenta una cuidada selección de artículos de largo formato, videos, ensayos fotográficos y entradas de blog de medios internacionales que destacan por su rigor y profundidad. Esta edición reúne 10 contenidos de temática variada que abarcan desde reportajes sobre desastres naturales y movimientos sociales hasta análisis de fenómenos culturales contemporáneos y avances científicos, pensados para lectores que buscan información más allá de los titulares breves de las redes sociales.

[MULTIMEDIA: https://storage.ghost.io/c/51/f8/51f871d8-b6be-4a73-b958-0ca4fff0110a/content/images/size/w1200/2026/07/tehran.jpg]

Entre los contenidos más destacados se encuentra el reportaje del fotógrafo Kevin Cooley para el Los Angeles Times, que documenta las especies vegetales que sobrevivieron a los incendios que devastaron Altadena, California el pasado año; la crónica de Whitney Washington para Burnaway sobre su recorrido por la ruta de colchas de Alabama, un proyecto de arte comunitario que recorre el estado; y la investigación de Christin El-Kholy para New Lines Magazine sobre la decisión del Comité para la Protección de Periodistas de eliminar 20 nombres de su lista de miembros de la prensa palestina fallecidos por acciones israelíes desde el 7 de octubre. También destaca el nuevo libro de Emmaia Gelman, analizado por Michael Arria en Mondoweiss, que desvela el historial de espionaje de la Liga Anti-Difamación contra activistas y movimientos palestinos; el reportaje de Rebecca Barker para Rewire News Group sobre Womontown, el enclave queer autosuficiente creado por un grupo de lesbianas en un barrio de Kansas City en los años 90; y la cobertura de Imogen West-Knights para Slate del Torneo de Crucigramas Profesional de Estados Unidos, celebrado en un hotel de Connecticut.

[MULTIMEDIA: https://storage.ghost.io/c/51/f8/51f871d8-b6be-4a73-b958-0ca4fff0110a/content/images/size/w600/2026/06/The2025SFArtBookFair_photobyLizzyMyers.jpg]

La recopilación incluye además contenidos de gran interés para el sector cultural español, como la celebración del décimo aniversario de la Feria de Arte de San Francisco, referente internacional del arte independiente que atrae cada año a decenas de profesionales españoles; la reapertura de plazos de solicitud de la Academia Cranbrook, una de las instituciones de formación artística más prestigiosas del mundo, para el curso de otoño de 2026; y análisis de fenómenos culturales globales como el reality Love Island, estudiado por la periodista Anna Peele en LitHub, o el estudio de Jenny Lehmann para Discover Magazine que data la risa humana en 15 millones de años. No faltan tampoco contenidos de historia LGBTIQ+, como la investigación de Amanda Timpson sobre la doble vida del gerente del icónico bar Stonewall Inn, cuna de los disturbios que dieron origen al movimiento de liberación gay moderno, y un análisis del impacto cultural y deportivo del futbolista Erling Haaland.

[MULTIMEDIA: https://storage.ghost.io/c/51/f8/51f871d8-b6be-4a73-b958-0ca4fff0110a/content/images/size/w600/2026/06/Irene-Perez-by-PD-Rearick.jpg]

fullArticle:
Meta publicly launched a new version of Muse Spark on Thursday, a multimodal AI model designed for agentic coding that aims to compete with similar products offered by OpenAI and Anthropic.

Spark 1.1, the first version of which was announced in April, can engage in multistep reasoning and handle complex processes, manage digital workflows, and deploy new features in enterprise systems, the company says.

Meta is a bit behind its competitors here; Anthropic and OpenAI have offered similar models for quite some time. But that doesn’t mean Meta’s entry into the market isn’t a threat.

An ongoing source of competitiveness within the AI industry remains the cost of usage, and Meta appears to be offering a competitive rate. Reuters reports that the company will charge $1.25 per million input to...

fullArticle:
Bernadette Scalora has been renting for more than three decades in south-east Queensland. (ABC News: Sarah Richards)

About one in three Queenslanders with disability surveyed say they lived in a home that didn't meet their needs last year.

Peak advocacy groups for the disability and real estate sectors have launched a campaign to support renters with disability to request home modifications.

Housing Minister Sam O'Connor welcomes the initiatives and says too many people with disability face barriers in finding housing.

Bernadette Scalora has rented six homes over three decades, and it has not always been easy to find one that accommodates her disability.

"Housing has always been one of my particular bugbears as a person with a disability," the 52-year-old said.

fullArticle:
TAMPA, Fla. — Environmental and scientific groups are calling for a halt to processing orbital data center applications, arguing the rush to move computing infrastructure into space has raised the stakes in a broader push for more megaconstellation oversight.

The coalition, represented by nonprofit public interest environmental law organization Earthjustice, petitioned the U.S. Federal Communications Commission July 8 to conduct a thorough environmental review before moving forward with licenses.

Specifically, the petition calls for a Programmatic Environmental Impact Statement (PEIS) of orbital data centers under the National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA), a 1969 law mandating federal agencies assess the environmental impacts of their actions.

title: Noskova: “Non riesco ancora a crederci. Con Muchova ci siamo conosciute davvero alle Olimpiadi”
summary: La ceca batte Kostyuk in due set e raggiunge la prima finale Slam della carriera: “Sull’erba sto usando molto il mio gioco. A questi livelli la differenza la fanno le piccolezze”

fullArticle:
Linda Noskova continua il suo splendido Wimbledon e raggiunge la prima finale Slam della carriera. La ceca ha battuto Marta Kostyuk con un doppio 6-4, confermando la solidità mostrata per tutto il torneo e costruendo il successo soprattutto intorno al servizio, diventato una delle armi decisive del suo tennis sull’erba.

In finale troverà la connazionale Karolina Muchova, per un derby ceco che conferma ancora una volta il rapporto speciale tra la Repubblica Ceca e Wimbledon. In conferenza stampa Noskova ha parlato delle emozioni ancora difficili da realizzare, del suo rapporto con Muchova, della tradizione ceca sui prati londinesi e del percorso mentale che l’ha portata a questa finale.

Linda, sei approdata alla tua prima finale Slam. Come ti senti?

LINDA NOSKOVA: Mi sento… Non so davver...

The greatest time-travel movie of all time is now No. 3 on HBO Max worldwide.

Time travel has fascinated filmmakers for decades, evolving from clever sci-fi concepts into stories packed with heart, humor, and mind-bending twists. Classics like The Terminator (1984), 12 Monkeys (1995), Primer (2004), Looper (2012), and Edge of Tomorrow (2014) all brought something unique to the genre, while Back to the Future (1985) remains the gold standard thanks to its perfect blend of adventure, comedy, unforgettable characters, and brilliantly constructed storytelling. Now, the greatest time-travel movie of all time is seeing a resurgence on streaming.

fullArticle:
En el marco de la presentación de los nuevos carriles exclusivos de autobuses en Nueva York, el presidente de la Autoridad Metropolitana del Transporte (MTA, por sus siglas en inglés), Janno Lieber, agradeció al alcalde Zohran Mamdani, pero también apuntó contra Eric Adams. En sus declaraciones, sostuvo que con la administración anterior la agencia no contaba con “apoyo local” y remarcó la buena relación con la gestión actual.

En el anuncio difundido por la oficina de Mamdani, Lieber celebró el nuevo plan de transporte, Next Stop: Fast Buses, Better Service, como parte de una alianza entre la MTA y el Departamento de Transporte de la ciudad (NYC DOT, en inglés). Luego, cuestionó el manejo de la problemática por parte de la administración del exalcalde Adams.

title: Why Every AI Agent Eventually Becomes an Operating System
summary:
Every AI Agent Eventually Becomes an Operating System

When I first started building AI agents, I thought the hard part would be making the model intelligent.

I was wrong.

The intelligence was the easiest part.

The real challenge appeared the moment the agent needed to do something.

It Always Starts Simple

Almost every AI project begins with something like this:

User
   │
   ▼
LLM
   │
   ▼
Response

It works.

Until the first real request arrives.

"Deploy this application."

The model can explain how.

It can't actually do it.

So you add tool calling.

Then You Add More

One feature becomes two.

Then five.

Then twenty.

Eventually your architecture starts looking more like this:

  • Tool calling
  • File system access
  • Terminal execution
  • Memory
  • Multi-agent workflows
  • API integrations
  • Plugin systems
  • Skills
  • Scheduling
  • Background tasks
  • Context management

At first these feel like unrelated features.

But they're not.

They're all trying to solve the same problem.

Turn reasoning into action.

The Shift Happens Quietly

One day you realize your project isn't really a chatbot anymore.

It's coordinating components.

One part plans.

Another remembers.

Another executes.

Another verifies.

Another communicates with external services.

The language model becomes only one component inside a much larger system.

That's when the architecture changes completely.

You're No Longer Building Around the Model

Many people design systems like this:

Everything
     │
     ▼
    LLM

Eventually it becomes difficult to maintain.

Instead, I started thinking of the model as one participant inside an execution pipeline.

Request
   │
   ▼
Planner
   │
   ▼
Capabilities
   │
   ▼
Execution
   │
   ▼
Verification
   │
   ▼
Response

The language model still makes decisions.

It just isn't responsible for everything anymore.

Integrations Aren't Features

One lesson surprised me.

Adding another integration rarely makes an agent significantly more capable.

Adding better architecture does.

For example, an agent might support:

  • Direct APIs
  • MCP servers
  • Local tools
  • Code execution
  • Shell execution

Those are all different ways of reaching the outside world.

The important question isn't:

"How many integrations do we support?"

It's:

"How does the agent decide which capability is appropriate?"

That becomes an architecture problem rather than an integration problem.

Context Is More Important Than Capability

A powerful tool that is never selected is effectively useless.

A lightweight tool chosen at the right time often produces better results.

This completely changed how I think about AI systems.

Instead of adding more features, I spend more time improving:

  • planning
  • decision making
  • capability discovery
  • execution flow
  • feedback loops

The result is usually a smarter system without changing the model itself.

AI Agents Are Slowly Becoming Operating Environments

Traditional software waits for instructions.

Modern AI systems coordinate them.

They're managing tools, memory, workflows, external services, files, and multiple execution paths simultaneously.

That feels much closer to an operating environment than a chatbot.

The language model is becoming the interface—not the entire application.

Final Thoughts

Every AI project seems to follow the same path.

You start with prompts.

Then tools.

Then memory.

Then workflows.

Then multiple agents.

Eventually you stop asking:

"How can I make the model smarter?"

And start asking:

"How can I build a better system around it?"

That mindset shift has probably influenced my projects more than any new model release over the past year.

I'm curious whether others building AI agents have noticed the same pattern, or if you've taken a completely different architectural approach.

fullArticle:
When you think about New Balance, you think about dad shoes. Those good old chunky 2000s runners loved by dads in Ohio and semi-ironically worn by just about everyone else are what NB’s all about.

The American brand is an authority in clunky elderly sneakers, but clunky elderly sandals? Not so much. Or at least that was the case up until this summer.

Someone in the New Balance HQ clearly decided this’ll be the year that New Balance’s sandal game catches up to its sneakers, because all of the brand's best new shoes are strap-wielding slip-ons.

Conveniently for New Balance, it already had a huge archive to work with, which has resulted in its normal slides becoming wavy see-through statement pieces and, in the case of the brand's newest viral sandal, the MT2, a barefoot 2010s MT10 model b...

title: Google Hands Open Health Stack To the Linux Foundation
summary:
BrianFagioli writes: The Linux Foundation intends to launch the Open Health Stack Software Foundation, a new vendor-neutral home for the Google Open Health Stack project. Google is contributing the project code and assets while Google.org is providing a $3 million grant. The initiative is also backed by Microsoft, Anthropic, and the World Health Organization, with the goal of building open source, AI-ready digital health infrastructure. Will moving the project under Linux Foundation governance accelerate adoption, or is this simply another foundation that most developers will never interact with? The new project will focus on core HL7 FHIR technologies for healthcare interoperability, the Open Health Stack Player deployment toolkit, and AI Commons -- a model-agnostic healthcare AI initiative being co-developed with the World Health Organization. A notable part of the announcement is its planned Implementer Program, which aims to give startups, small businesses, and local developers in low- and middle-income countries a formal role in governance. In other words, the effort is not just about building healthcare software standards, but about making sure the people implementing them in underserved markets help shape the project too.

Read more of this story at Slashdot.

title: Building a Driver-Based Cooldown Manager for Laravel
summary:
Most Laravel applications eventually need some form of cooldown.

Not request throttling—but actual action cooldowns.

Examples include:

  • Password reset requests
  • Email verification
  • OTP / SMS sending
  • AI prompt generation
  • Report exports
  • Payment retries
  • Reward claiming
  • Expensive background jobs

Laravel already provides an excellent RateLimiter, but it's primarily designed for request throttling (for example, "60 requests per minute").

When I was building an application, I found myself needing something slightly different:

  • Persist cooldowns in either cache or database
  • Attach cooldowns directly to Eloquent models
  • Reuse the same API across controllers, jobs, commands, and middleware
  • Store cooldowns for arbitrary targets such as Users, API tokens, IP addresses, or custom identifiers
  • Allow switching storage drivers without changing application code

That led me to build Laravel Cooldown.

The Goal

I wanted an API that was expressive enough to read almost like English.

Cooldown::for('password_reset', $user)->enforce();

// Perform the action...

Cooldown::for('password_reset', $user)->for(300);

Checking a cooldown is equally straightforward:

if (Cooldown::for('password_reset', $user)->active()) {
    $remaining = Cooldown::for('password_reset', $user)
        ->info()
        ->remainingSeconds();
}

Some Design Decisions

Rather than coupling everything to a single storage implementation, the package uses Laravel's Illuminate\Support\Manager to provide interchangeable drivers.

Currently it supports:

  • Cache
  • Database

Additional drivers can be registered using Laravel's familiar extend() pattern.

Another design decision was to support Eloquent models as first-class cooldown targets.

Instead of manually generating cache keys, you can simply write:

$user->cooldown('email_verification')->for(300);

The package automatically resolves unique keys for models, scalars, IP addresses, and other supported targets.

Middleware That Only Starts on Success

One small behavior that bothered me with many cooldown implementations is that they start counting down before the request has actually succeeded.

Imagine submitting a form that fails validation.

You shouldn't have to wait another minute just because you mistyped your email address.

The middleware in Laravel Cooldown only creates the cooldown after successful (2xx) or redirected (3xx) responses.

Features

  • Cache and Database drivers
  • Expressive fluent API
  • Native Eloquent integration
  • Route middleware
  • Immutable DTOs
  • Event dispatching
  • Automatic database pruning
  • Custom driver support

Feedback Welcome

This is an open-source project, and I'd really appreciate feedback from the Laravel community.

If you have suggestions for the API, architecture, or documentation—or ideas for additional drivers or features—I'd love to hear them.

GitHub:
https://github.com/zaber-dev/laravel-cooldown

fullArticle:
The New York Times and The Daily News claim that OpenAI has been lying about its ability to search customer chat log data and training datasets for their copyrighted works. It’s the latest escalation in a two-year-old lawsuit against the AI firm for allegedly violating copyright law by training its generative AI models on the Times’ content and reproducing that journalism in user outputs.

Throughout the case, OpenAI has argued that it lacked the ability to search its own training corpus. It also argued that searching or producing its massive collection of ChatGPT conversations would be technically burdensome and would raise user-privacy concerns because the logs would need to be retrieved, processed, and de-identified. The outlets sought that data to determine whether their copyrighted jo...

If you are feeling financially depleted personally, and pessimistic about the economic outlook generally, figures released this week won't cheer your mood.

The OECD found Australia was one of 11 developed countries where the real minimum wage had fallen between April 2025 and April 2026.

Meanwhile, Deloitte Access Economics in its latest Business Outlook cut its forecast for growth in 2026–27, with the dismal message: "The economy is now expected to limp along at less than 2.0 per cent annual growth for the next two years."

This was followed by the International Monetary Fund's World Economic Outlook data projecting Australia's GDP growth to be 1.9 per cent this year and 1.7 per cent next year....

fullArticle:
Slate Auto, the EV startup backed by Jeff Bezos, has built its business around the idea of simplicity and customization. Start with a $24,950 unpainted gray electric truck with 205 miles of range — and none of the technological flair — and let the buyer customize it to their liking through vehicle wraps, decals, lighting, and other accessories.

The vehicle wraps, printed vinyl film that can be applied to a car, truck, or SUV without needing to paint it, are a central component of this customization strategy with hundreds of different colors and combinations. And now, Slate is adding even more through a partnership with crayon maker Crayola.

fullArticle:
Rusia ha construido en los territorios ucranianos bajo su control un sistema institucionalizado de adoctrinamiento y militarización de menores que podría constituir un crimen de lesa humanidad. Esa es la conclusión central del informe presentado este jueves en Viena por expertos independientes designados por la Organización para la Seguridad y la Cooperación en Europa (OSCE), que estima en 1,6 millones el número de niños y jóvenes ucranianos expuestos a esas prácticas en Crimea y en las regiones orientales parcialmente controladas por Moscú.

El documento fue elaborado bajo el denominado Mecanismo de Moscú, un instrumento de la OSCE que permite a un grupo de Estados miembros encargar a expertos independientes la investigación de vulneraciones graves de los derechos humanos en un país parti...

Jorge 'Yuri' Carot, jugador de la cantera taronja de 2,08 metros y 20 años de edad, emigra también a la NCAA

El canterano del Valencia Basket Jorge 'Yuri' Carot jugará la próxima campaña en la liga universitaria de Estados Unidos (NCAA) pero el club 'taronja' mantendrá sus derechos en Europa, según pudo saber EFE por fuentes conocedoras del acuerdo.

Carot, que llegó a debutar con el primer equipo del Valencia Basket y este curso ha sido una de las referencias del filial en la Liga U22, ha firmado un contrato con la Universidad del Pacífico, con cuyo equipo disputará la próxima campaña.

El jugador de Alboraia (Valencia) no ha depositado su cláusula de rescisión sino que ha llegado a un acuerdo con el Valencia Basket en virtud del cual sus derechos en Europa seguirán siendo del club,

fullArticle:
When architect Nick Varey received the brief for Moraine House, one request stopped him in his tracks. For more than two decades, the clients, a couple based primarily outside Cambridge, made use of their two-bedroom, two-bathroom Marylebone flat—located in a Portman Estate mansion block dating back to the 1890s—a few nights a week mainly for work. While in town, they habitually took to the city’s vast culinary scene. A kitchen, they reasoned, was simply unnecessary. It was a first for Varey.

He probed, prodded, and eventually brought the clients around when he discovered that family and friends also stay there and might occasionally want to dine in. Melded discreetly within the open-plan living space and presented more as furniture than function, a single run of low, handleless cabinetry...

title: Carlyle logra una rentabilidad cinco veces mayor al vender su unidad de energía para centros de datos a EQT por 2.600 millones de dólares
summary: La firma de capital privado Carlyle ha cerrado la venta de su división de energía para centros de datos a la empresa sueca EQT por 2.600 millones de dólares, obteniendo una rentabilidad cinco veces superior a su inversión inicial, en un contexto de fuerte demanda de infraestructura para inteligencia artificial.
imageAlt: Carlyle logra una rentabilidad cinco veces mayor al vender su unidad de energía para centros de datos a EQT por 2.600 millones de dólares
aiSummary: La firma de capital privado Carlyle ha cerrado la venta de su división de energía para centros de datos a la empresa sueca EQT por 2.600 millones de dólares, obteniendo una rentabilidad cinco veces superior a su inversión inicial, en un contexto de fuerte demanda de infraestructura para inteligencia artificial.
keyPoints: Carlyle vende su unidad de energía para centros de datos a la firma de inversión EQT por un valor total de 2.600 millones de dólares.
La operación le reporta a Carlyle una rentabilidad cinco veces superior a la inversión inicial realizada en la división.
La venta se enmarca en un contexto de demanda récord de infraestructura tecnológica para soportar el crecimiento de la inteligencia artificial.
La operación supone un punto de inflexión positivo para las ventas de carteras de capital privado en el sector tecnológico.
whyMatters: Esta operación evidencia el enorme crecimiento del mercado de infraestructura para inteligencia artificial, un sector que está atrayendo inversiones masivas de capital privado a nivel global. Para el ecosistema tecnológico europeo, la entrada de EQT en este segmento refuerza la apuesta por la soberanía digital y la expansión de centros de datos sostenibles en la región. Además, la alta rentabilidad obtenida por Carlyle envía una señal clara a otros inversores sobre el potencial de retorno de los activos vinculados a la IA, lo que podría acelerar la entrada de nuevos capitales en este nicho en los próximos años, con repercusiones directas en la competitividad de empresas españolas y europeas que dependen de estos servicios.

multimedia.alt: Carlyle logra una rentabilidad cinco veces mayor al vender su unidad de energía para centros de datos a EQT por 2.600 millones de dólares
multimedia.caption: Imagen de portada

fullArticle:
La firma de capital privado estadounidense Carlyle ha cerrado la venta de su división especializada en sistemas de energía para centros de datos a la empresa de inversión sueca EQT por un valor total de 2.600 millones de dólares, obteniendo una rentabilidad cinco veces superior a la inversión inicial realizada en la unidad. La operación, anunciada en el marco de la estrategia de desinversión de activos no estratégicos de Carlyle, se enmarca en un contexto de demanda sin precedentes de infraestructura tecnológica para soportar el crecimiento exponencial de la inteligencia artificial y la expansión de servicios en la nube a nivel global.

La división vendida, dedicada al desarrollo y mantenimiento de sistemas de alimentación ininterrumpida y gestión energética eficiente para centros de datos, se había convertido en uno de los activos más valiosos de la cartera de Carlyle en el sector tecnológico. La fuerte demanda de este tipo de soluciones, impulsada por la necesidad de garantizar el funcionamiento continuo de instalaciones que alojan servidores de IA de alto consumo energético, ha disparado el valor de estos activos en los últimos dos años. Para el mercado europeo, la entrada de EQT en este segmento supone un impulso a la inversión en infraestructura digital sostenible en la región, con posibles repercusiones positivas para empresas españolas del sector tecnológico que dependen de estos servicios para su operativa diaria.

fullArticle:
Fifteen years is pretty old for an American whiskey; in fact, most bourbon is bottled at somewhere between four and seven years old. Tequila, on the other hand, usually spends less than three years in barrels. And even when it’s labeled as extra añejo, five years is often the upper limit. Código 1530, however, decided to age its new expression for a full 15 years before giving it a relatively brief finish, making this one of the oldest (and most expensive) tequilas currently on the market.

Código 1530 is technically a celebrity-backed brand, although that’s something it leans on for some releases more than others. It was founded in in 2017 by the former CEO of Crocs Ron Snyder, distiller Federico Vaughn, Betsy Heneghan, and country music star George Strait. In 2022, the brand was acquired...

title: La Reserva Federal presenta los equipos de trabajo y metas para reformar la conducción de su política monetaria
summary: La Reserva Federal de Estados Unidos creó cinco grupos de trabajo co-liderados por expertos externos para evaluar y mejorar sus métodos, herramientas analíticas y enfoques de política monetaria, con el objetivo de cumplir su mandato de estabilidad de precios y empleo máximo en un contexto de cambios económicos sin precedentes.
imageAlt: La Reserva Federal presenta los equipos de trabajo y metas para reformar la conducción de su política monetaria
aiSummary: La Reserva Federal de Estados Unidos creó cinco grupos de trabajo co-liderados por expertos externos para evaluar y mejorar sus métodos, herramientas analíticas y enfoques de política monetaria, con el objetivo de cumplir su mandato de estabilidad de precios y empleo máximo en un contexto de cambios económicos sin precedentes.
keyPoints: La Reserva Federal creó cinco grupos de trabajo especializados para evaluar y mejorar los métodos, herramientas analíticas y enfoques de su política monetaria.
Los equipos están co-liderados por economistas externos de prestigio, líderes empresariales y exfuncionarios de bancos centrales con experiencia demostrada en sus áreas.
Los grupos operarán de forma independiente, con el mandato de analizar evidencias, emitir informes rigurosos y presentar recomendaciones al Comité Federal de Mercado Abierto.
El objetivo principal es garantizar que la Fed esté preparada para cumplir sus objetivos de estabilidad de precios y empleo máximo en un contexto de transformaciones económicas sin precedentes.
whyMatters: Esta iniciativa de la Reserva Federal tiene un impacto estratégico directo en la economía global, y por ende en España y la zona euro, ya que las decisiones de política monetaria estadounidense afectan los tipos de cambio, los flujos de inversión y las condiciones de financiación en todo el mundo. La revisión de los métodos de la Fed podría derivar en cambios en su estrategia de tipos de interés, lo que influirá en la competitividad de las exportaciones españolas, el coste de la deuda pública y privada, y la estabilidad de los mercados financieros europeos. Además, el modelo de trabajo colaborativo con expertos externos que implementa la Fed puede servir de referencia para el Banco Central Europeo en sus propios procesos de modernización de la política monetaria, por lo que los resultados de estos grupos de trabajo serán seguidos de cerca por las autoridades económicas españolas y europeas.

multimedia.alt: La Reserva Federal presenta los equipos de trabajo y metas para reformar la conducción de su política monetaria
multimedia.caption: Imagen de portada

fullArticle:
La Reserva Federal de Estados Unidos anunció este jueves la composición y los objetivos de cinco grupos de trabajo especializados creados para impulsar la modernización de la conducción de su política monetaria, en un contexto de transformaciones económicas profundas que han modificado el funcionamiento de los mercados y la relación entre las variables macroeconómicas en las últimas décadas. El presidente de la entidad, Kevin Warsh, destacó que el compromiso de la Fed con la estabilidad de precios y el máximo empleo es 'inquebrantable', y que la revisión de sus métodos busca garantizar que la institución cumpla su mandato con la mayor rigurosidad posible en un momento de cambios sin precedentes.

Los equipos estarán co-liderados por asesores externos de reconocido prestigio: economistas de primer nivel, líderes empresariales y exfuncionarios de bancos centrales con experiencia demostrada en sus áreas de especialización, y contarán con el apoyo de personal de la propia Reserva Federal. Operarán de forma completamente independiente, con el mandato de analizar todas las evidencias disponibles, emitir informes rigurosos y presentar recomendaciones directas al Comité Federal de Mercado Abierto, el órgano encargado de definir la política monetaria estadounidense.

La iniciativa busca evaluar si las herramientas analíticas, los enfoques de política y los métodos de actuación de la Fed pueden ser mejorados para adaptarse a la nueva realidad económica, marcada por fenómenos como la inflación postpandemia, las perturbaciones en las cadenas de suministro globales y la digitalización de los mercados financieros. Los resultados de estos grupos de trabajo serán publicados de forma periódica en el sitio web oficial de la Reserva Federal, y sus conclusiones podrían sentar las bases de cambios profundos en la estrategia de política monetaria de la principal economía del mundo, con repercusiones directas en los mercados globales y en la economía española y europea.

multimedia.alt: Is Syria stable enough to engage with the world?
multimedia.caption: Is Syria stable enough to engage with the world?

fullArticle:
The Trump administration plans to remove Syria from a list of state sponsors of ‘terrorism’.

It’s now a year and a half since the Bashar al-Assad regime was overthrown in Syria.

Interim President Ahmed al-Sharaa has visited countries in the region as well as the US, as his transitional government tries to bring Syria out of diplomatic and economic isolation.

Washington has lifted sanctions and plans to remove Damascus from the list of state sponsors of “terrorism”, a designation that dates back to 1979.

But security challenges continue – the latest being twin attacks in the capital this month.

There is also pressure from Israel, which has occupied additional territory in the southwest of Syria since the Assad regime was deposed.

Omar Abu Laila – Political and Syrian affairs analyst

title: Call Of Duty: Black Ops 1 And 2 Ports Are Out Now On PS5, But DLC Costs Extra
summary:
The ports of Call of Duty: Black Ops (2010) and Black Ops 2 (2012) are available now. The ports, which were confirmed in June, are developed by Iron Galaxy in conjunction with Activision.

As announced previously, these are not remasters or remakes--if you enjoyed the games in their original states, that's what you're getting here.

The games cost $40, but PlayStation Plus members can get each for $20 through August 6. There is no bundle that puts them together.

The descriptions for each state that the games offer "a classic Call of Duty experience," and as such, cross-platform multiplayer is not supported. Beyond the base games, Activision is also selling each game's season pass for $30 (or $10 through August 6 for PlayStation Plus members). The season pass bundles include additional multiplayer maps and Zombies content.

The next brand-new Call of Duty game is Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4, which launches this October for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, Switch 2, and PC.

OpenAI released an all-new ChatGPT desktop app today, which includes a new ChatGPT Work agent alongside ChatGPT Codex.

With the new ChatGPT desktop app, OpenAI now has a desktop app with robust browser capabilities. ChatGPT (and Codex before it) also supports a Chrome desktop browser plugin. This lets Chrome users benefit from ChatGPT integration without completely switching browsers.

More than half of U.S. states—29 and counting—have now reported cases of a parasitic illness that can induce weeks of watery, explosive diarrhea since the wave first emerged in May. But this mysterious outbreak, a poop tsunami, as some might say, appears to have a new epicenter.

Michigan, the Great Lakes state whose license plates have long advertised the nickname “Water Wonderland,” has now documented 992 cases of this prolific diarrhea-generating infection, according to the latest update from the Michigan Department of Health and Human Services (MDHHS). fullArticle:
A veteran of the fight game, legendary promoter Bob Arum has overseen many of the sport’s most iconic moments, including the comeback of Muhammad Ali, but there is another occasion that sticks out as the proudest of his career.

Arum’s first promotional gig came when he staged Ali’s 1972 dust-up with George Chuvalo, the year after Ali lost out to Joe Frazier in the ‘Fight of the Century’ when attempting to regain the heavyweight throne.

Under Arum’s guidance, Ali went on to avenge that defeat to Frazier and then dethrone George Foreman in the fabled ‘Rumble in the Jungle’ – arguably the most famous event in the history of the sport – to become a two-time world champion.

fullArticle:
There's no doubt about it: the skies are filling up with satellites that regularly pass overhead, creating light trails that can interfere with stargazing, backyard astronomy, astronomical research, and pose a significant threat to natural ecosystems. The problem stems from the design of the satellites themselves, which include solar arrays that reflect sunlight, creating an artificial, diffuse glow that brightens the night sky and obscures the light of natural objects (the Moon, the planets, and stars).

With 1.7 million satellites expected to be launched into orbit in the coming years, things may reach the point where children look up and wonder why the Moon and the few remaining natural objects still visible are not streaking across the night sky. To mitigate this problem, a team of res...

iHeartMedia is entering a consent decree with the Federal Communications Commission over the regulatory organization’s payola investigation, the FCC announced on Thursday.

The FCC first announced a probe against iHeart last year over what it called “showola,” investigating claims that iHeart gave increased airplay to artists who played the radio conglomerate’s concerts and music festivals.

In its initial reply to the FCC, iHeart denied leveraging airplay for live performances, telling the FCC that “musicians perform at iHeart events for the promotional value, not for additional airplay on Company Stations.”

title: Três bugs que cometi construindo um sistema de confiabilidade (e os três fingiram que deu tudo certo)
summary:
Passei os últimos dias construindo o HookSafe, uma camada que fica entre a plataforma de pagamento e o servidor do cliente para garantir que nenhum webhook se perca. A promessa do produto é uma só: se o seu servidor cair, eu seguro o evento e insisto até entregar.

Cometi três bugs no caminho. O que me fez escrever este texto não foi a burrice de cada um, foi perceber, depois, que os três tinham a mesma forma: todos faziam uma falha parecer um sucesso. Num sistema cujo produto é confiabilidade, é difícil imaginar categoria de bug mais cruel.

Bug 1: engoli o erro, e o sistema jurou que tinha entregue

A função que entrega o evento no servidor do cliente ficou assim:

go
resposta, err := clienteHTTP.Do(requisicao)
if err != nil {
return "", nil // <- olhe com carinho
}

Eu quis escrever return "", err. Escrevi nil.

O efeito: apontei o destino para uma porta onde não havia nada escutando. O Do devolveu um belo connection refused. E a minha função respondeu ao worker: "sem erro, chefe". O worker, obediente, marcou o evento como entregue, com o status da resposta vazio, e seguiu a vida.

No banco:
id | pedido_id | status | tentativas | resposta
----+-----------+----------+------------+----------
6 | 9002 | entregue | 0 |

Um evento que nunca saiu do lugar, registrado como entregue. Se isso estivesse em produção, um cliente teria pagado, não receberia nada, e o meu painel mostraria, orgulhoso, que a entrega foi um sucesso.

Aquele if err != nil { return err } que a gente reclama de repetir em Go existe exatamente por isso. A linguagem te obriga a decidir o que fazer com a falha, toda vez. O preço da verbosidade é que ninguém engole um erro sem querer... a menos que digite nil.

Bug 2: o log mentiu

Corrigi o primeiro bug, rodei de novo, e o worker começou a cuspir isto, a cada cinco segundos, para sempre:

worker: erro ao marcar morto 7: ERROR: column "reposta" does not exist
worker: evento 7 esgotou as tentativas, marcado como MORTO

Leia as duas linhas de novo. A primeira diz que falhou ao gravar. A segunda anuncia que gravou.

O código:

go
if erroBanco := repo.MarkDead(ctx, evento.ID, descricao); erroBanco != nil {
log.Printf("erro ao marcar morto %d: %v", evento.ID, erroBanco)
}
log.Printf("evento %d esgotou as tentativas, marcado como MORTO", evento.ID)

Faltou um continue. O log de sucesso rodava sempre, tivesse a operação funcionado ou não.

O bug original era bobo: escrevi reposta em vez de resposta num UPDATE. Mas o reposta só me custou um minuto depois que eu vi a mensagem de erro. O que quase me custou uma hora foi o log seguinte, afirmando com todas as letras que o evento tinha sido marcado como morto. Eu fui olhar o banco procurando entender por que um evento "morto" continuava sendo processado, quando a resposta estava na linha de cima da minha própria tela.

Um log que mente é pior que a ausência de log. Sem log você sabe que não sabe. Com um log mentiroso, você depura com confiança na direção errada.

E o efeito colateral era divertido: como o MarkDead falhava, o evento nunca saía do status falhou, o next_retry_at já tinha vencido, e o worker o pegava de novo, e de novo, e de novo. Um loop infinito construído a partir de um "s" faltando.

Bug 3: cinco nanossegundos

O worker deveria acordar a cada cinco segundos. Configurei assim:

go
WorkerInterval: lerDuracao("WORKER_INTERVAL", 5),

E o log saiu assim:

23:00:09 worker: procurando eventos pendentes...
23:00:09 worker: procurando eventos pendentes...
23:00:09 worker: procurando eventos pendentes...
[mais algumas centenas de milhares de linhas no mesmo segundo]

time.Duration, em Go, é um int64 que conta nanossegundos. O literal 5, num lugar que espera uma time.Duration, vira time.Duration(5), que é cinco nanossegundos. O certo é 5 * time.Second, porque time.Second é a constante que vale um bilhão.

Esse é o mais inofensivo dos três, e o único que se anuncia na cara. Mas ele tem um parente perigoso: se você lê a variável de ambiente com time.ParseDuration, o valor precisa vir com unidade ("5s"). Se alguém setar WORKER_INTERVAL=5, o parse falha, o código cai no valor padrão em silêncio, e você fica sem entender por que a configuração não pega.

Ou seja, mesmo o bug bobinho tem uma versão dele que se disfarça de sucesso.

O padrão

Escrevi os três esperando que o texto virasse uma lista de descuidos. Virou outra coisa.

O erro engolido transformou uma falha de rede em uma entrega bem-sucedida. O log mentiroso transformou um UPDATE que não aconteceu em uma confirmação. O parse silencioso transforma uma configuração inválida em um valor padrão. Nenhum dos três derruba o sistema. Todos os três fazem o sistema afirmar com confiança algo que não é verdade.

Software que quebra é chato. Software que mente é caro. E existe uma classe inteira de bugs, os mais difíceis de achar, cuja assinatura é exatamente essa: o caminho de falha existe, mas ele foi escrito para parecer o caminho de sucesso.

O antídoto não é elegante. É desconfiar de todo lugar onde você decide, você mesmo, o que significa "deu certo": todo err que você não propaga, todo log de sucesso que não está guardado atrás de uma verificação, todo valor padrão que substitui uma entrada inválida sem avisar.

Se o seu sistema promete confiabilidade, ele precisa começar sendo honesto consigo mesmo.

O código é aberto: github.com/Rupelio/hooksafe. É um projeto de estudo, e as decisões de arquitetura estão registradas em ADRs no repositório, incluindo a chave de idempotência, que errei duas vezes antes de acertar. Mas essa é outra história.

Software que quebra é chato. Software que mente é caro. E existe uma classe inteira de bugs, os mais difíceis de achar, cuja assinatura é exatamente essa: o caminho de falha existe, mas ele foi escrito para parecer o caminho de sucesso.

O antídoto não é elegante. É desconfiar de todo lugar onde você decide, você mesmo, o que significa "deu certo": todo err que você não propaga, todo log de sucesso que não está guardado atrás de uma verificação, todo valor padrão que substitui uma entrada inválida sem avisar.

Se o seu sistema promete confiabilidade, ele precisa começar sendo honesto consigo mesmo.

O código é aberto: github.com/Rupelio/hooksafe. É um projeto de estudo, e as decisões de arquitetura estão registradas em ADRs no repositório, incluindo a chave de idempotência, que errei duas vezes antes de acertar. Mas essa é outra história.
keyPoints: Três bugs que cometi construindo um sistema de confiabilidade (e os três fingiram que deu tudo certo)
whyMatters: Noticia de actualidad en el sector de tecnologia.

multimedia.alt: Três bugs que cometi construindo um sistema de confiabilidade (e os três fingiram que deu tudo certo)
multimedia.caption: Imagen de portada del artículo

multimedia.alt: pic
multimedia.caption: pic

fullArticle:
Passei os últimos dias construindo o HookSafe, uma camada que fica entre a plataforma de pagamento e o servidor do cliente para garantir que nenhum webhook se perca. A promessa do produto é uma só: se o seu servidor cair, eu seguro o evento e insisto até entregar.

Cometi três bugs no caminho. O que me fez escrever este texto não foi a burrice de cada um, foi perceber, depois, que os três tinham a mesma forma: todos faziam uma falha parecer um sucesso. Num sistema cujo produto é confiabilidade, é difícil imaginar categoria de bug mais cruel.

A função que entrega o evento no servidor do cliente ficou assim:

go
resposta, err := clienteHTTP.Do(requisicao)
if err != nil {
    return "", nil   // <- olhe com carinho
}

Eu quis escrever return "", err. Escrevi nil.

O efeito: apontei o destino...

fullArticle:
Google is rolling out a new feature aimed at helping people understand when an ad they’re seeing was made using AI technology.

AI makes it easier for businesses to create ads, place their brand’s products in various settings, and save money on real-world e-commerce photography. But it can also be misleading if consumers don’t know that what they’re looking at isn’t a real product photo. While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that’s something Google only required election ads to disclose.

Hamas has shifted much of its organizational center of gravity toward Türkiye in recent months, according to meetings, activities and public positions by the group, after years in which it kept its operations there at a distance and reduced its presence.

The shift has coincided with statements of condemnation and solidarity after bombings in Syria, whose new government Hamas is seeking to approach.

The clearest sign of Hamas’s growing reliance on Ankara came in May, when the group chose Türkiye as the venue for internal elections to select the head of its political bureau. The vote ended without a decisive result.

A new drama series about elite golfers on an exclusive island is coming to Netflix.

Titled “Jupiter Island,” the show hails from Connor Hines, the creator of FX’s “Love Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. & Carolyn Bessette,” and A24. The project, a straight-to-series order, is the first in a new overall deal between Hines and the streamer.

Per the logline, “On the ultra-exclusive Jupiter Island, the privileged world of professional golfers and their wives fractures when a mysterious outsider arrives — and a secret threatens golf’s reigning power couple.” Casting has yet to be announced.

fullArticle:
LOS ANGELES — Frozen mid-leap, the soccer star Kylian Mbappé is projected onto a massive, larger-than-life screen. Upon closer inspection, the monumental image commanding the Los Angeles County Museum of Art’s (LACMA) Resnick Pavilion is a tiny, one-inch (~2.5 cm) sculpture. Its body, carefully crafted from a Wrigley’s chewing gum wrapper, sits beneath a camera that projects its image in real time onto a screen. This installation is the work of Lyndon J. Barrois, Sr., whose solo exhibition Fútbol Life: Animated Sportraits is currently on view.

The show arrives in Los Angeles at a pivotal moment, with SoFi Stadium hosting FIFA World Cup matches, including a quarter-final match set to take place tomorrow, July 10. For this exhibition, Barrois recreated crucial moments from FIFA history dati...

fullArticle:
La LNH a validé ce jeudi le calendrier de la saison 2026-2027, une saison qui marquera un tournant historique pour le championnat de France avec l'entrée en vigueur d'un tout nouveau format de compétition.

Les seize clubs engagés lanceront les hostilités le week-end des 4, 5 et 6 septembre 2026, pour 30 journées de saison régulière. Un marathon qui déterminera non plus seulement un champion direct, mais bien une hiérarchie qualificative pour la grande nouveauté de l'exercice : les playoffs !

À l'issue de la phase régulière, les équipes classées de la 5e à la 8e place s'affronteront en playoffs pour tenter d'arracher le dernier ticket qualificatif pour une compétition européenne. Un dispositif qui devrait maintenir sous tension une bonne partie du plateau jusqu'aux dernières journées, loi...

fullArticle:
Yes, soccer is a mainstream American sport.

The World Cup games involving the U.S. team had large TV audiences. The rest of them did well, too.

Via Austin Karp of Sports Business Journal, the 16 games played in the single-elimination Round of 32 averaged 16.9 million U.S. viewers, between the English-language broadcast on Fox and the Spanish-language version on Telemundo and Peacock.

Fox and FS1 contributed 9.5 million to the number. Telemundo and Peacock averaged 7.4 million.

The average for the Spanish broadcast was split equally between linear and streaming. A whopping 3.7 million on average watched the games on Peacock.

Cuando falta espacio en casa, resulta tentador dejar cosas en rincones, junto a la cama o al pie del armario. Aun así, ese recurso recarga visualmente las habitaciones y dificulta mantener el orden.

Heather Aiello, fundadora y directora ejecutiva de The Organized You, recordó al medio citado que el piso es un espacio valioso dentro de la vivienda. Cuando los objetos acaban allí, añadió, los ambientes se perciben más desordenados y cuesta más conservarlos.

Casi todos los participantes, el 98,6%, presentaban al menos un hallazgo genético y, en la mayoría de los casos, los resultados requerían seguimiento. El estudio, publicado en Genetics in Medicine, también analiza más detenidamente qué hace falta para convertir esos hallazgos en un seguimiento asistencial adecuado.
